proven.claim: PROVE: orderTotal at line 22 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice * quantity) products.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: orderTotal at line 22 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice * quantity) products.
; Two-item cart model. If Z3 returns unsat the identity holds unconditionally.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const bp2 Real)
(declare-const qty2 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal2 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: loop body — lineTotal = basePrice * quantity
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
(assert (= lineTotal2 (* bp2 qty2)))
; Transition: accumulator — orderTotal += lineTotal each iteration
(assert (= orderTotal (+ lineTotal1 lineTotal2)))
; Negate: orderTotal differs from the algebraic sum
(assert (not (= orderTotal (+ (* bp1 qty1) (* bp2 qty2)))))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — orderTotal is exactly the sum of lineTotals; no rounding or omission

proven.claim: PROVE: If all basePrices and quantities are >= 0, then orderTotal >= 0 at line 22.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: If all basePrices and quantities are >= 0, then orderTotal >= 0 at line 22.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const bp2 Real)
(declare-const qty2 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal2 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Preconditions: all inputs non-negative
(assert (>= bp1 0.0))
(assert (>= qty1 0.0))
(assert (>= bp2 0.0))
(assert (>= qty2 0.0))
; Transitions: loop body
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
(assert (= lineTotal2 (* bp2 qty2)))
(assert (= orderTotal (+ lineTotal1 lineTotal2)))
; Negate: orderTotal is negative despite non-negative inputs
(assert (< orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: unsat — non-negative prices and quantities guarantee a non-negative total

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3, P5
; REACHABLE: placeOrder never validates item.basePrice >= 0.
; A negative price reverses the contribution of a lineTotal, making orderTotal negative.
; This is reachable via any caller supplying a cart with a discounted or erroneous item.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: loop body — lineTotal = basePrice * quantity
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
; Transition: single-item cart — orderTotal = 0 + lineTotal1
(assert (= orderTotal lineTotal1))
; Code does NOT validate basePrice >= 0
; Code DOES NOT constrain bp1 before the multiply
(assert (< bp1 0.0))
(assert (> qty1 0.0))
; Consequence: orderTotal is negative — line 22 logs a negative dollar amount
(assert (< orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — witness e.g. bp1 = -5.0, qty1 = 2.0, orderTotal = -10.0
;         line 22 logs "Order total for <customer>: $-10.00"

; PRINCIPLE: P3, P5
; REACHABLE: placeOrder never validates item.quantity >= 0.
; TypeScript `number` admits negative values; no guard exists before the multiply.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: loop body — lineTotal = basePrice * quantity
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
; Transition: single-item cart
(assert (= orderTotal lineTotal1))
; Code does NOT validate quantity >= 0
(assert (> bp1 0.0))
(assert (< qty1 0.0))
; Consequence: negative quantity inverts a positive price — orderTotal < 0
(assert (< orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — witness e.g. bp1 = 20.0, qty1 = -3.0, orderTotal = -60.0

violations.claim: REACHABLE: placeOrder calls checkAvailability(item.productId) and stores the
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; REACHABLE: placeOrder calls checkAvailability(item.productId) and stores the
; result in `available`, but `available` is never read afterward.
; The loop body computes lineTotal = basePrice * quantity WITHOUT referencing available.
; Existing contract [P6] proves the stub db always returns available = 0.
; Therefore a nonzero orderTotal is logged at line 22 even when stock = 0.
(declare-const available Real)
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: checkAvailability returns 0 (proven by existing [P6] contract)
(assert (= available 0.0))
; Transition: loop body computes lineTotal WITHOUT using `available`
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
(assert (= orderTotal lineTotal1))
; Realistic inputs: positive price and quantity
(assert (> bp1 0.0))
(assert (> qty1 0.0))
; Consequence: orderTotal > 0 while available = 0
(assert (> orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — line 22 logs a positive order total for items with zero stock;
;         execution continues to the reserveStock loop, which will overdraw inventory

violations.claim: REACHABLE: cart is caller-supplied, length is unchecked.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE: cart is caller-supplied, length is unchecked.
; If cart is empty the loop body never executes and orderTotal stays at its
; initialization value of 0. The log fires, then the second loop runs for 0 items.
(declare-const cart_length Int)
(declare-const orderTotal_init Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Transition: orderTotal initialized to 0 before the loop
(assert (= orderTotal_init 0.0))
; cart is empty — loop body executes zero times, no assignment to orderTotal
(assert (= cart_length 0))
; Consequence: orderTotal is unchanged from its initialization
(assert (= orderTotal orderTotal_init))
; The log at line 22 fires with a $0.00 total
(assert (= orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — an empty-cart call reaches line 22 logging "$0.00";
;         the order is semantically a no-op but execution proceeds normally

violations.claim: REACHABLE: quantity = 0 is not rejected. The multiply by zero collapses the
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE: quantity = 0 is not rejected. The multiply by zero collapses the
; entire basePrice contribution regardless of its value.
; The item remains in the cart and will be passed to reserveStock after line 22.
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const lineTotal1 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
; Code does NOT validate quantity > 0
; Transition: lineTotal = basePrice * 0
(assert (= qty1 0.0))
(assert (= lineTotal1 (* bp1 qty1)))
; Transition: single-item cart accumulation
(assert (= orderTotal lineTotal1))
; Inputs: nonzero price — the zero-contribution is not from a free item, it is a data error
(assert (> bp1 0.0))
; Consequence: lineTotal is zero regardless of price; orderTotal = 0
(assert (= lineTotal1 0.0))
(assert (= orderTotal 0.0))
(check-sat)
; expect: sat — witness: bp1 = 49.99, qty1 = 0, lineTotal = 0, orderTotal = 0
;         reserveStock will later be called with quantity = 0 (a no-op write, proven reachable
;         by existing [P6] contract on reserveStock)

proven.claim: Prove orderTotal cannot have changed between line 22 and line 27.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; Prove orderTotal cannot have changed between line 22 and line 27.
; Between the two log statements only reserveStock calls execute — no
; assignment to orderTotal exists in that code span.
(declare-const orderTotal_at_22 Real)
(declare-const orderTotal_at_27 Real)

; Model the code transition: loop 2 performs only reserveStock calls,
; leaving orderTotal untouched.
(assert (= orderTotal_at_27 orderTotal_at_22))

; Negate the property: attempt to find a state where orderTotal changed.
(assert (not (= orderTotal_at_27 orderTotal_at_22)))
(check-sat)
; unsat -> orderTotal is invariant across loop 2; line 27 reflects the
;          exact value computed in loop 1.

proven.claim: Prove reserve_calls == cart_length — no iteration is skipped or added.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; Prove reserve_calls == cart_length — no iteration is skipped or added.
(declare-const cart_length Int)
(declare-const reserve_calls Int)

; Code transition: the for-loop body executes unconditionally once per element.
(assert (>= cart_length 0))
(assert (= reserve_calls cart_length))

; Negate the property.
(assert (not (= reserve_calls cart_length)))
(check-sat)
; unsat -> the second loop delivers exactly cart.length reserveStock calls.

violations.claim: placeOrder calls checkAvailability then discards the result.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; placeOrder calls checkAvailability then discards the result.
; reserveStock requires quantity <= available, but placeOrder never checks this.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available_after Int)

; checkAvailability contract (existing proven property P5): available >= 0
(assert (>= available 0))

; Cart item has a positive quantity — a real order.
(assert (> quantity 0))

; Code transition: placeOrder makes NO comparison before loop 2.
; reserveStock executes: available_after = available - quantity
(assert (= available_after (- available quantity)))

; Violation condition: overbooking — more reserved than was available.
(assert (< available_after 0))
(check-sat)
; sat -> the log at line 27 can fire after a reservation that drove
;        available negative, because the availability guard was never applied.

violations.claim: Two cart items may alias the same productId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; Two cart items may alias the same productId.
; The first reserveStock call mutates DB state that the second call reads.
(declare-const available_initial Int)
(declare-const reserved_initial Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const available_after_1 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_1 Int)
(declare-const available_after_2 Int)

; Initial state from DB.
(assert (>= available_initial 0))
(assert (>= reserved_initial 0))

; Both quantities are positive.
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (> quantity_2 0))

; First reserveStock call (item 1, productId X):
;   available_after_1 = available_initial - quantity_1
;   reserved_after_1  = reserved_initial  + quantity_1
(assert (= available_after_1 (- available_initial quantity_1)))
(assert (= reserved_after_1  (+ reserved_initial  quantity_1)))

; First call succeeds (enough stock for item 1 alone).
(assert (<= quantity_1 available_initial))

; Second reserveStock call (item 2, SAME productId X):
;   reads available_after_1 (the mutated value), not available_initial
;   available_after_2 = available_after_1 - quantity_2
(assert (= available_after_2 (- available_after_1 quantity_2)))

; Violation: second reservation drives available negative.
(assert (< available_after_2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat -> line 27 fires "2 items reserved" after the second call has made
;        available negative; witness: available=3, q1=2, q2=2 -> after2=-1

violations.claim: item.quantity is caller-supplied; placeOrder validates nothing.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; item.quantity is caller-supplied; placeOrder validates nothing.
; reserveStock has no guard either (proven in existing contracts).
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const reserved Int)
(declare-const available_after Int)
(declare-const reserved_after Int)

; Code transition: quantity flows directly from CartItem into reserveStock,
; which computes: available_after = available - quantity
;                 reserved_after  = reserved  + quantity
(assert (>= available 0))
(assert (>= reserved 0))
(assert (< quantity 0))   ; negative quantity — no guard prevents this
(assert (= available_after (- available quantity)))
(assert (= reserved_after  (+ reserved  quantity)))

; Violation: reserved goes negative (stock "un-reserved").
(assert (< reserved_after 0))
(check-sat)
; sat -> a negative quantity causes reserveStock to decrement reserved,
;        and line 27 logs this corrupted state as a successful placement.

violations.claim: cart.length counts CartItem array slots, not distinct productIds.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; cart.length counts CartItem array slots, not distinct productIds.
; When two items alias the same productId, available stock is doubly consumed
; yet the log reports "2 items reserved" for a single product.
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const quantity_1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_2 Int)
(declare-const available_after_both Int)
(declare-const cart_length Int)

; Two-item cart sharing one productId.
(assert (= cart_length 2))
(assert (> quantity_1 0))
(assert (> quantity_2 0))
(assert (>= available 0))

; Code transition: two sequential reserveStock calls on the same row.
(assert (= available_after_both (- (- available quantity_1) quantity_2)))

; Violation: log says "2 items reserved" but available is negative —
;            the single product is over-reserved.
(assert (< available_after_both 0))
(check-sat)
; sat -> "2 items reserved" is logged while the single product's available
;        balance is negative; the message is semantically misleading.

violations.claim: The stub db.getAvailable always returns 0 (existing proven contract).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; The stub db.getAvailable always returns 0 (existing proven contract).
; reserveStock computes available_after = 0 - quantity and writes it.
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const available Int)
(declare-const available_after Int)

; Code transition: stub forces available=0, reserveStock subtracts quantity.
(assert (= available 0))        ; stub invariant
(assert (> quantity 0))         ; cart item has positive quantity
(assert (= available_after (- available quantity)))

; Violation: negative available is stored before line 27 fires.
(assert (< available_after 0))
(check-sat)
; sat -> with the stub in place, every positive-quantity cart item causes
;        reserveStock to write a negative available value; line 27 logs
;        "reserved" over already-negative stock for every single item.

proven.claim: PROVE: refundAmount at line 37 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice_i * quantity_i)
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: refundAmount at line 37 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice_i * quantity_i)
; Strategy: model the two-iteration accumulation, negate the sum identity → expect unsat
(declare-const bp0 Real)
(declare-const qty0 Real)
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const r0 Real)
(declare-const r1 Real)
; Code: refundAmount = 0 initially
; After item 0: r0 = 0 + bp0 * qty0
(assert (= r0 (* bp0 qty0)))
; After item 1: r1 = r0 + bp1 * qty1
(assert (= r1 (+ r0 (* bp1 qty1))))
; Negate: r1 != bp0*qty0 + bp1*qty1
(assert (not (= r1 (+ (* bp0 qty0) (* bp1 qty1)))))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — refundAmount is provably the exact algebraic sum of all lineTotals

proven.claim: PROVE: all basePrice >= 0 AND all quantity >= 0 implies refundAmount >= 0 at line 37
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; PROVE: all basePrice >= 0 AND all quantity >= 0 implies refundAmount >= 0 at line 37
; Strategy: assert non-negative preconditions + accumulation → negate property → expect unsat
(declare-const bp0 Real)
(declare-const qty0 Real)
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const refundAmount Real)
; Preconditions: both basePrices and quantities are non-negative
(assert (>= bp0 0.0))
(assert (>= qty0 0.0))
(assert (>= bp1 0.0))
(assert (>= qty1 0.0))
; Code transition: accumulation
(assert (= refundAmount (+ (* bp0 qty0) (* bp1 qty1))))
; Negate: refundAmount < 0
(assert (< refundAmount 0.0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — non-negative inputs provably yield non-negative refundAmount

violations.claim: REACHABLE: item.basePrice is unchecked — a negative basePrice makes refundAmount negative at line 37
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3, P5
; REACHABLE: item.basePrice is unchecked — a negative basePrice makes refundAmount negative at line 37
; processRefund performs no validation of item.basePrice before the accumulation loop
(declare-const basePrice Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const refundAmount Real)
; Code transition: refundAmount accumulates basePrice * quantity for one item
(assert (= refundAmount (* basePrice quantity)))
; TypeScript number admits negatives; no guard exists in processRefund
(assert (< basePrice 0.0))
(assert (> quantity 0.0))
; Violation: refundAmount < 0 — customer is net-charged on a "refund"
(assert (< refundAmount 0.0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. basePrice=-5, quantity=2 → refundAmount=-10 is logged as $-10.00

violations.claim: REACHABLE: item.quantity is unchecked — negative quantity makes refundAmount negative at line 37
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3, P5
; REACHABLE: item.quantity is unchecked — negative quantity makes refundAmount negative at line 37
; processRefund performs no validation of item.quantity before accumulation
(declare-const basePrice Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const refundAmount Real)
; Code transition: single-item accumulation
(assert (= refundAmount (* basePrice quantity)))
; TypeScript number admits negatives; no guard on quantity
(assert (> basePrice 0.0))
(assert (< quantity 0.0))
; Violation: refundAmount < 0
(assert (< refundAmount 0.0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. basePrice=20, quantity=-1 → refundAmount=-20, logged as $-20.00
; This also propagates to releaseStock(productId, -1) after line 37 (existing proven contract P3)

violations.claim: REACHABLE: both basePrice < 0 AND quantity < 0 — double-negation inflates refundAmount
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; REACHABLE: both basePrice < 0 AND quantity < 0 — double-negation inflates refundAmount
; semantically incorrectly; processRefund has no sign guards
(declare-const basePrice Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const lineContribution Real)
; Code transition: single-item product
(assert (= lineContribution (* basePrice quantity)))
; Both inputs are negative (unchecked by processRefund)
(assert (< basePrice 0.0))
(assert (< quantity 0.0))
; Violation: product of two negatives is positive — semantically meaningless refund inflation
(assert (> lineContribution 0.0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. basePrice=-10, quantity=-3 → contribution=+30, refundAmount inflated
; No guard exists to reject or normalise double-negative items before line 37

violations.claim: REACHABLE: item.basePrice = 0 — multiplication-by-zero collapses line contribution to 0
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; REACHABLE: item.basePrice = 0 — multiplication-by-zero collapses line contribution to 0
; processRefund has no guard requiring basePrice > 0
(declare-const basePrice Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const lineContribution Real)
(declare-const refundAmount Real)
; Code transition: lineContribution = basePrice * quantity
(assert (= lineContribution (* basePrice quantity)))
; Even with a valid non-zero quantity, zero basePrice nullifies the entire contribution
(assert (= basePrice 0.0))
(assert (> quantity 0.0))
; Transition: refundAmount accumulates this zero contribution
(assert (= refundAmount lineContribution))
; Violation: refundAmount = 0 despite a non-zero quantity item being present
(assert (= refundAmount 0.0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — a free item silently produces a $0 contribution; stock IS still released post-37
; The asymmetry (stock released, $0 refunded) is semantically invalid; no guard exists

violations.claim: REACHABLE: CartItem.basePrice is a mutable TypeScript object field —
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5, P2
; REACHABLE: CartItem.basePrice is a mutable TypeScript object field —
; external mutation between placeOrder and processRefund decouples refundAmount from orderTotal
; processRefund has no snapshot or immutability guarantee on order.items
(declare-const basePrice_at_order Real)
(declare-const basePrice_at_refund Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const orderTotal Real)
(declare-const refundAmount Real)
; placeOrder computed orderTotal from the basePrice at order time
(assert (> basePrice_at_order 0.0))
(assert (> quantity 0.0))
(assert (= orderTotal (* basePrice_at_order quantity)))
; basePrice mutated externally between the two calls (valid TypeScript — interface fields are mutable)
(assert (> basePrice_at_refund 0.0))
(assert (not (= basePrice_at_order basePrice_at_refund)))
; processRefund reads the current (post-mutation) basePrice at line 35
(assert (= refundAmount (* basePrice_at_refund quantity)))
; Violation: refundAmount != orderTotal at line 37 — over- or under-refund
(assert (not (= refundAmount orderTotal)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — e.g. ordered at basePrice=100, refunded at basePrice=150 → $50 over-refund
; No snapshot, lock, or equality check guards against this divergence

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: refundAmount at line 37 equals the algebraic sum of all (basePrice_i * quantity_i)
; Strategy: model the two-iteration accumulation, negate the sum identity → expect unsat

(declare-const qty0 Real)
(declare-const bp1 Real)
(declare-const qty1 Real)
(declare-const r0 Real)
(declare-const r1 Real)
; Code: refundAmount = 0 initially
; After item 0: r0 = 0 + bp0 * qty0

; After item 1: r1 = r0 + bp1 * qty1

; Negate: r1 != bp0*qty0 + bp1*qty1

proven.claim: PROVE: releaseStock is called exactly order.items.length times before line 43.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; PROVE: releaseStock is called exactly order.items.length times before line 43.
; The loop at lines 39-41 has no break, continue, or conditional skip.
; Strategy: model the loop transition, negate the count equality, expect unsat.
(declare-const items_length Int)
(declare-const release_calls Int)
; Precondition: array length is non-negative
(assert (>= items_length 0))
; Transition: the for-of loop body executes unconditionally once per element
(assert (= release_calls items_length))
; Negate: release_calls != items_length
(assert (not (= release_calls items_length)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → the call count equals the item count in every execution

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: processRefund has no idempotency guard.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: processRefund has no idempotency guard.
; It never reads order.status before executing. A second call on the same order
; re-enters the releaseStock loop at line 40 and reaches line 43 again.
; The second release drives the DB reserved counter negative.
; Transition modeled: two sequential releaseStock(same productId, quantity) calls.
(declare-const reserved_initial Int)
(declare-const quantity Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_first Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_second Int)
; First call: preconditions hold, executes correctly
(assert (> quantity 0))
(assert (>= reserved_initial 0))
(assert (<= quantity reserved_initial))
(assert (= reserved_after_first (- reserved_initial quantity)))
; processRefund has no guard on order.status — second call proceeds unconditionally
; Second call: same quantity against the already-reduced reserved counter
(assert (= reserved_after_second (- reserved_after_first quantity)))
; Violation: reserved goes negative, confirming the double-refund is reachable
(assert (< reserved_after_second 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → double-refund is reachable. Witness: reserved_initial=3, quantity=3
;        after first: 0, after second: -3

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: Two items in order.items can alias the same productId.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: Two items in order.items can alias the same productId.
; processRefund does not check for duplicate productIds before the release loop.
; Both iterations call releaseStock on the same underlying reserved counter.
; Transition modeled: two releaseStock calls on the same product's reserved counter.
(declare-const reserved_initial Int)
(declare-const quantity_item1 Int)
(declare-const quantity_item2 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_item1 Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_item2 Int)
; Both items are valid individually
(assert (> quantity_item1 0))
(assert (> quantity_item2 0))
(assert (>= reserved_initial 0))
; First iteration's releaseStock: decrements shared reserved counter
(assert (= reserved_after_item1 (- reserved_initial quantity_item1)))
; Second iteration's releaseStock on same productId: decrements again from reduced counter
(assert (= reserved_after_item2 (- reserved_after_item1 quantity_item2)))
; Violation: combined release exceeds original reservation
(assert (< reserved_after_item2 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → aliased items exhaust reserved. Witness: reserved_initial=3, q1=2, q2=2
;        after item1: 1, after item2: -1

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: releaseStock requires quantity <= reserved (domain invariant).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: releaseStock requires quantity <= reserved (domain invariant).
; processRefund establishes NO such relationship before calling it at line 40.
; item.quantity is caller-supplied; processRefund performs zero validation.
; By line 43, the DB holds the negative value written during the release loop.
; Transition modeled: releaseStock(productId, quantity) where quantity > reserved.
(declare-const reserved_db Int)
(declare-const item_quantity Int)
(declare-const reserved_after_release Int)
; Code path: db.getReserved returns reserved_db, releaseStock subtracts unconditionally
(assert (>= reserved_db 0))
(assert (> item_quantity 0))
; processRefund never reads reserved_db — no guard before the call
; Transition: releaseStock computes reserved - quantity with no floor check
(assert (= reserved_after_release (- reserved_db item_quantity)))
; processRefund passes item_quantity directly from the CartItem without validation
; item_quantity > reserved_db is reachable (e.g. partial reservation or no prior reserve)
(assert (> item_quantity reserved_db))
(assert (< reserved_after_release 0))
(check-sat)
; sat → negative reserved is written to DB before line 43 is reached.
;        Witness: reserved_db=2, item_quantity=5 → reserved_after=-3

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: refundAmount is recomputed from live CartItem fields at refund time.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: refundAmount is recomputed from live CartItem fields at refund time.
; CartItem.basePrice is a mutable TypeScript field — processRefund does not compare
; to what placeOrder charged. If basePrice changed between order and refund, the customer
; receives a refund for a different amount than they were charged.
; Transition modeled: placeOrder computes orderTotal, then basePrice is mutated, then
; processRefund computes refundAmount from the mutated value.
(declare-const base_price_at_order Real)
(declare-const base_price_at_refund Real)
(declare-const quantity Real)
(declare-const order_total Real)
(declare-const refund_amount Real)
; placeOrder's transition: orderTotal = basePrice_original * quantity
(assert (> base_price_at_order 0.0))
(assert (> quantity 0.0))
(assert (= order_total (* base_price_at_order quantity)))
; TypeScript CartItem fields are mutable — basePrice can change between calls
(assert (not (= base_price_at_refund base_price_at_order)))
(assert (> base_price_at_refund 0.0))
; processRefund's transition: refundAmount = basePrice_current * quantity
(assert (= refund_amount (* base_price_at_refund quantity)))
; Violation: refundAmount != orderTotal at line 43
(assert (not (= refund_amount order_total)))
(check-sat)
; sat → mutated basePrice means refundAmount diverges from original payment.
;        A discounted item could be refunded at full price (or vice versa).

(assert (= release_calls items_length))

(assert (not (= release_calls items_length)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → the call count equals the item count in every execution
